body:
### Problem

After updating to 0.12 on one of my pies, I noticed that syntax highlighting with treesitter no longer worked. I was able to reproduce this by building a 32bit x86 version on my machine as well. Bisecting this showed 0977f70f4dd3d14175 as being the problematic commit. 
See also https://github.com/neovim/neovim/actions/runs/24207453801/job/70666980562?pr=38917#logs

### Steps to reproduce

- Build nvim in 32bit mode
- Try to open a lua file which should autoamtically used the bundled treesitter parser

### Expected behavior

Syntax highlighting is working, in this case there is no highlighting at all.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.13.0-dev-142+g1d1b3c8e58

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

no treesitter

### Operating system/version

Debian

### Terminal name/version

kitty

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-256color

### Installation

build from repo

comment_data.body:
That indicates that some operation(s) in the internal range module are vulnerable to overflow (which can trigger much earlier on 32bit systems). Since we don't have such a system to test ourselves and you run a build from source, can you add some `vim.print` debugging to the relevant functions and see what goes wrong in your case?

(This may be an issue with PUC Lua on 32bit, too. We only make sure that Nvim compiles with PUC Lua but don't run tests in CI.)

You can do the same tests in a docker container running with a 32bit debian, this is how I reproduced it on my notebook. Of course I can try debugging this as well, but I would need some pointers of where to start.

You can see which functions were used in the change you linked, especially the newly added `intersection` and `clip_regions` one; I would start with those, which should be easy to test in isolation (try a few inputs, see if the result is correct).

It's also helpful to confirm that it's indeed a 32 bit issue, not simply a PUC Lua issue. (I see a couple of `#` length operators used, which behave differently between PUC Lua and LuaJIT if the table has holes; so that's something to look for as well.)
Yeah, it's not as simple as that. What about `vim._maxint` (which is 4294967295 on my system)? Can you try replacing `math.huge` with that in `languagetree.lua`?
Maybe I am doing something wrong but :lua vim.print(vim._maxint) returns "inf" as well, both on my 64bit system and inside the 32bit docker container.

Hmm, not for me (and definitely shouldn't on 64bit; it's literally `2^32-1`, which is definitely representable).

Can you test that change?
sorry, on my 64bit system it shows "nil" and on 32bit "inf"
4294967295 but changing this in the range does not fix it. printint r1 still shows -1 -1 -1

You can try it yourself in docker, just run it from inside the neovim folder
``` 
docker run -v $PWD:/build --rm -it --platform linux/386 debian:stable bash
```
And then install the build tools and build and install it. Then you can reproduce it easily

I can't use docker, so I need to rely on your help here.

Another thing: I cannot build against PUC Lua locally, so can you please test such a build on your 64bit host? (The instructions are in BUILD.md.)

I get the same issue with the PUC version. The 64bit build is ok, the 32bit build has no syntax highlighting, There is apparently still an overflow since I see -1 -1 -1 for R1 again in the intersection call.

Just to make sure that this is indeed the reason why syntax highlighting does not work, I added the following hack to M.intersection

```
if (r1[4] == -1) then
 r1[4] = math.huge
 r1[5] = math.huge
 r1[6] = math.huge
end
```
and this fixed the issue for me. Of course this is not a proper fix but I have troubles figuring out where these range values are coming from in the first place.
Look in line 1123 of `languagetree.lua`; there's a `line + 1` that could conceivably overflow.
I just checked it, these values seems fine, they are not overflowing.
Yeah, maybe the overflow happens in `vim.treesitter._range`, e.g., in the `unpack4` function (which also does "pointer arithmetic").

labels.description: wrong behavior that was introduced in a previous commit (please bisect)
labels.name: bug-regression

labels.description: issue has been tracked to a specific commit
labels.name: has:bisected

title: treesitter highlighting does not work on 32bit machines with v0.12
